summary: Poland's published material is so far explanatory rather than directive. NASK, the national research institute and CSIRT operator, names post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution as responses to the quantum threat, and the National Institute of Telecommunications has produced introductory material and a seminar. No national post-quantum migration policy or timeline is attested in the gathered documents.
govActors:
NASK | national research institute and CSIRT operator, presents post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution as responses to quantum threats
Instytut Łączności (National Institute of Telecommunications) | state research institute, introductory material and a seminar on post-quantum cryptography
provenance: NASK names PQC and QKD as responses to quantum threats: NASK QKD presentation (ingested)
Introductory explainer and seminar: Instytut Lacznosci items (ingested) - T4 research-institute material
RAD-on project profile and NASK Cyberpolicy US-strategy analysis: ingested but flagged as a research-database record and as analysis of another country, not used for posture - REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: no Polish standards camp or national policy attested in captured summaries
summary/standardFamilies/algorithms/hybridDeployment/timeline/targetCompletion omitted or minimal: no national migration policy stated in captured summaries
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
